title: Command-Line Completion
subtitle: {key:Tab} to complete; {key:Ctrl-D} to list.
summary: {key:Tab} completes commands, options, filenames, and tags depending on context. {key:Ctrl-D} lists matches. {key:Ctrl-A} inserts all matches. The wildmenu makes {key:Tab} cycle through choices.

blocks.text: Vim knows what kind of argument the current command expects, and completes accordingly. {key:Tab} is context-sensitive: it completes Ex command names, option names, filenames, tag names, and more.

blocks.title: What Gets Completed Where
blocks.text:
Vim infers context from the command:

  • `:e {Tab}` → filenames in cwd.
  • `:set {Tab}` → option names.
  • `:help {Tab}` → help tags.
  • `:colorscheme {Tab}` → installed colorschemes.
  • `:command {Tab}` → user commands.

The `wildmode` option controls how {key:Tab} behaves:

  • `full` — cycle one match per {key:Tab}.
  • `longest` — first complete to longest common prefix.
  • `list` — show all matches without completing.
  • Combined: `:set wildmode=longest:full,full` — first {key:Tab} completes to longest unique prefix and shows menu, second cycles.

`wildmenu` enables the *visual* completion menu — a status-line bar of choices that {key:Tab} walks through.
Typing `:e ` and then pressing {key:Ctrl-D} is a fast way to list all files in the cwd that match what you've typed so far.
